articles.headline: Fourth World Cup still has South Korea captain Son Heung-Min feeling 'like a kid'
articles.description: South Korea captain Son Heung-min says he feels like a "kid" as he enters his fourth World Cup, where he will debut against the Czech Republic in a Group A match in Guadalajara on Thursday.

images.name: Son Heung-Min [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min was all smiles on Wednesday at South Korea's final training session before their 2026 FIFA World Cup opener against Czechia.
images.credit: Ulises RUIZ / AFP via Getty Images

articles.headline: Will 2026 prove to be Son Heung-Min's final FIFA World Cup hurrah?
articles.description: Son Heung-Min's previous three World Cups perfectly mirrored where he was in his career. So what does 2026 hold for him?

images.name: Will the 2026 World Cup be Son Heung-Min's last hurrah? [1500x844]
Son Heung-Min 2014 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min's first World Cup goal came in South Korea's ill-fated 2014 campaign -- where they picked up just one point from three games in their worst showing since 1998.
images.credit: ADRIAN DENNIS / AFP via Getty Images

images.name: Son Heung-Min 2018 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min's memorable sealer in a stunning 2-0 win over Germany in 2018 ultimately proved in vain as South Korea narrowly failed to make it out of the group stage.

images.name: Son Heung-Min 2022 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: South Korea finally reached the round of 16 at the 2022 FIFA World Cup for the first time in 12 years -- although that was where their campaign would end with a 4-1 loss to Brazil.

images.name: Will Mexico or South Korea top Group A at the World Cup?
images.caption: Gab & Juls debate who will win Group A between Mexico, South Korea, Czechia and South Africa at the FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Japan have a plan to win the World Cup by 2050. How close will they get in 2026?
articles.description: Japan's "100 Year Vision" is well ahead of schedule, but are the Samurai Blue ready win the World Cup now?

images.name: Japan World Cup chances [1500x844]
images.alt: Wataru Endo, Takefusa Kubo and Ritsu Doan

images.name: Wataru Endo [1296x729]
images.caption: Although he was sidelined by injury in February, Wataru Endo has recovered in time for this third FIFA World Cup -- and first as captain of Japan.

images.name: Japan vs. Brazil [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan's preparations for the 2026 FIFA World Cup saw them claim friendly victories over Brazil and England for the first time ever.

images.name: Have injuries ruined Japan's chances of causing World Cup upsets?
images.caption: Gabe Tan reacts to Japan's squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: World Cup final squads ranked: Of all 48 national teams, who can win this summer?
articles.description: We have the final 26-player squads that can win the FIFA World Cup, so let's go through all 48 teams and rank where they stand.

images.name: Pochettino: USMNT are moving in a good direction ahead of World Cup
images.caption: Mauricio Pochettino speaks after the USMNT's 3-2 win over Senegal ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Midfield metronome Hwang In-Beom could be key for South Korea at the 2026 FIFA World Cup
articles.description: Hwang In-Beom is not South Korea's most-prominent name. But he could be their most important player at the World Cup.

images.name: Hwang In-Beom [1296x729]
images.caption: Although he was initially a doubt after picking up an injury in March, Hwang In-Beom has been passed fit to feature for South Korea at the 2026 FIFA World Cup -- where he will likely play a key role for them in midfield.

images.name: Hwang In-Beom [1296x729]
images.caption: Having had plenty of previous experience in Europe with Rubin Kazan, Olympiacos and Red Star Belgrade, Hwang In-Beom has now spent the past two seasons in the Eredivisie with Feyenoord.

images.name: Son Heung-min set for fourth World Cup with South Korea
images.caption: South Korea name their 26-man squad for the 2026 World Cup, headlined by a fourth competition appearance for captain Son Heung-min.

articles.headline: Why Japan could be the most exciting team at the 2026 FIFA World Cup
articles.description: Even with a couple of key players out through injury, Japan could still be the most exciting team at the 2026 World Cup.

images.name: Japan [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan's preparation for the 2026 FIFA World Cup has seen them record victories over Brazil and England for the first time ever over the past seven months.

images.name: Kaoru Mitoma [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan were dealt a real blow ahead of the World Cup after injuy ruled out Kaoru Mitoma, who scored the only goal of the game when they beat England 1-0 at Wembley back in March.

images.name: Have injuries ruined Japan's chances of causing World Cup upsets?
images.caption: Gabe Tan reacts to Japan's squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

season.name: 2026 World Cup Qualifying - AFC, Second Round

article.headline: Kusini Yengi stars as Socceroos beat Palestine 5-0
article.description: Kusini Yengi scored twice as the Socceroos claimed an emphatic win over Palestine in Tuesday's World Cup qualifiers.
article.linkText: Yengi stars as Socceroos beat Palestine 5-0

images.name: Kusini Yengi  [1296x729]
images.caption: Kusini Yengi of the Socceroos celebrates after scoring his second goal against Palestine.

peers.name: Kusini Yengi  [1296x1296]
peers.caption: Kusini Yengi of the Socceroos celebrates after scoring his second goal against Palestine.

peers.name: Kusini Yengi  [1296x729]
peers.caption: Kusini Yengi of the Socceroos celebrates after scoring his second goal against Palestine.

peers.caption: Kusini Yengi of the Socceroos celebrates after scoring his second goal against Palestine.

peers.name: Kusini Yengi  [1296x518]
peers.caption: Kusini Yengi of the Socceroos celebrates after scoring his second goal against Palestine.

images.name: Nestory Irankunda scores first Socceroos goal in win over Palestine
images.caption: Nestory Irankunda caps the Socceroos' 5-0 victory in style after converting from the spot in a World Cup qualifier against Palestine.

article.story:
Socceroos forward Kusini Yengi scored two goals before Nestory Irankunda added the icing on the cake as Australia posted a 5-0 World Cup qualifier victory over Palestine in Perth.

Yengi opened the scoring in Tuesday night's clash via a fifth-minute penalty, and he made it 3-0 in the 41st when he produced some fancy footwork to bamboozle the Palestine defence.

The 25-year-old's beautiful through ball helped set up Martin Boyle for Australia's fourth goal in the 53rd minute in a comprehensive display from the home side.

Irankunda, who came on in the 70th minute for just his second international cap, scored via the penalty spot in the 88th minute.

The 18-year-old, who has signed with German giants Bayern Munich, celebrated his maiden Socceroos goal with a trademark backflip.

The result meant Australia ended this phase of qualifying with a perfect six wins from six outings, and a dominant 22-0 for-and-against goals record.

Australia were hoping for South Korea to either draw or lose to China in Group C on Tuesday night.

If that result had eventuated, Australia would have jumped ahead of South Korea in the world rankings, thus ensuring the Socceroos would be seeded third in Asia and avoid Japan and Iran in the next phase of qualifying.

South Korea ended up beating China 1-0, meaning the Socceroos will now have one of either Japan, South Korea or Iran in their group for the next phase.

Socceroos coach Graham Arnold made eight changes to his starting line-up from the team that beat Bangladesh 2-0 in Dhaka last week, with only goalkeeper Joe Gauci, Jackson Irvine and Yengi holding their spots.

A crowd of 18,261 fans attended Tuesday's match, and the Palestinian supporters easily drowned out their Australian counterparts.

Palestine's players were left fuming after the Socceroos were awarded a soft penalty in the third minute when Aziz Behich clattered into his opponent.

With no VAR available the penalty stood, and Yengi sent his shot low and to the left to narrowly evade the goalkeeper's outstretched glove.

Local fans entered the match hoping for some hometown magic, and Adam Taggart delivered it in the 26th minute with a nice one-two move with Martin Boyle.

Boyle controlled Taggart's lobbed pass with his head before squaring the ball back to the star Perth Glory striker, who made no mistake from close range.

Despite being 2-0 down, Palestine continued to attack hard whenever given the chance, forcing defender Alessandro Circati to make some important blocks.

Palestine's bad luck continued in the 41st minute when Boyle, who appeared to be in an offside position, set up Yengi's second goal.

Yengi's sweet footwork bamboozled his defender, allowing him to nail the goal from close range.

The 3-0 half-time scoreline wasn't enough to satisfy Australia, and Yengi's beautiful through ball helped set up a fourth goal in the 53rd minute.

Taggart latched onto Yengi's pass before unselfishly squaring across to Boyle for a simple tap-in.

It meant Boyle had two assists and a goal to his name.

Injury hit in the 57th minute when Taggart lunged for a goal, with the veteran striker gingerly limping off.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal!  Australia 1, Palestine 0. Kusini Yengi (Australia) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom left corner.
Goal!  Australia 2, Palestine 0. Adam Taggart (Australia) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Martin Boyle.
Amid Mahajna (Palestine) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Australia 3, Palestine 0. Kusini Yengi (Australia) left footed shot from very close range to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Martin Boyle with a through ball.
First Half ends, Australia 3, Palestine 0.
Keanu Baccus (Australia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Australia 4, Palestine 0. Martin Boyle (Australia) left footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Adam Taggart.
Substitution, Palestine. Oday Dabbagh replaces Omar Faraj.
Substitution, Palestine. Ameed Sawafta replaces Mohammed Rashid.
Substitution, Australia. Connor Metcalfe replaces Keanu Baccus.
Substitution, Australia. Apostolos Stamatelopoulos replaces Adam Taggart because of an injury.
Substitution, Palestine. Moustafa Zeidan replaces Jonathan Cantillana.
Substitution, Palestine. Muhamad Darwish replaces Amid Mahajna.
Connor Metcalfe (Australia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Australia. Nestory Irankunda replaces Martin Boyle.
Jackson Irvine (Australia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Palestine. Samer Jondi replaces Islam Batran.
Substitution, Australia. Jordan Bos replaces Kusini Yengi.
Substitution, Australia. Cameron Devlin replaces Mathew Leckie.
Goal!  Australia 5, Palestine 0. Nestory Irankunda (Australia) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom left corner.
